fr: Il ne sait pas de quoi il parle.
en: He doesn't know what he's talking about.
note: Savoir (to know a fact/how). Ne … pas wraps the verb. De quoi = 'what' in indirect questions.

fr: Ça ne m'étonne pas du tout.
en: That doesn't surprise me at all.
note: Étonner is used impersonally here. Du tout intensifies the negation — 'not at all'.

fr: Tu te rends compte de ce que tu dis ?
en: Do you realise what you're saying?
note: Se rendre compte de = to realise. Reflexive — the te agrees with the subject.

fr: On s'en fiche complètement.
en: We couldn't care less.
note: S'en ficher = not to care. En replaces the thing you don't care about. Very common in spoken French.

fr: Elle tient à ce que tout soit parfait.
en: She insists that everything be perfect.
note: Tenir à ce que triggers the subjonctif in the subordinate clause (soit).

fr: Je me demande si c'est vraiment nécessaire.
en: I wonder if that's really necessary.
note: Se demander si introduces an indirect yes/no question — no inversion needed.

fr: Ça dépend du contexte, bien sûr.
en: It depends on the context, of course.
note: Dépendre de — de + le contracts to du before a masculine noun.

fr: Nous n'avons pas les moyens de faire ça.
en: We can't afford to do that.
note: Avoir les moyens de = to be able to afford. Lit. 'to have the means to.'

fr: Il faut qu'on en parle sérieusement.
en: We need to talk about it seriously.
note: Il faut que always triggers subjonctif. On is used casually for 'we' in speech.

fr: Je n'y crois pas une seconde.
en: I don't believe it for a second.
note: Y croire — y replaces à + thing. Ne … pas une seconde is a strong negation.

fr: Elle finit toujours par avoir raison.
en: She always ends up being right.
note: Finir par + infinitif = to end up doing something. Very useful structure.

fr: Tu as l'air épuisé ce matin.
en: You look exhausted this morning.
note: Avoir l'air + adjectif = to look/seem. The adjective agrees with the subject.

fr: Il n'a rien dit pendant toute la réunion.
en: He didn't say anything throughout the entire meeting.
note: Ne … rien wraps the auxiliary. With passé composé, rien goes between auxiliary and past participle.

fr: Elle s'est rendu compte de son erreur trop tard.
en: She realised her mistake too late.
note: Reflexive verbs use être as auxiliary. Past participle of se rendre compte does NOT agree here (indirect reflexive).

fr: On a failli rater le train.
en: We almost missed the train.
note: Faillir + infinitif = to almost do something. Rater = to miss (informal). Manquer is more formal.

fr: Ils ont mis du temps à comprendre.
en: It took them a while to understand.
note: Mettre du temps à = to take time to. Mis is the irregular past participle of mettre.

fr: Je lui ai dit ce que je pensais.
en: I told him/her what I thought.
note: Lui = indirect object (to him/her). Ce que introduces an embedded clause.

fr: Nous avons décidé de prendre les choses en main.
en: We decided to take matters into our own hands.
note: Prendre en main = to take charge of. Décider de + infinitif is the standard construction.

fr: Elle a eu du mal à s'en remettre.
en: She had a hard time getting over it.
note: Avoir du mal à = to have difficulty. S'en remettre = to recover from something (from se remettre de).

fr: Le gouvernement a pris des mesures insuffisantes.
en: The government took insufficient measures.
note: Pris is the irregular past participle of prendre. Des mesures uses the partitive article.

fr: Il m'a regardé sans dire un mot.
en: He looked at me without saying a word.
note: Sans + infinitif = without doing. Regardé does not agree here — the direct object precedes but agreement only applies when it's a pronoun preceding the aux.

fr: On s'est disputés pour rien.
en: We argued over nothing.
note: Reflexive verbs use être. Disputés agrees with on (treated as plural here). Pour rien = over nothing / for nothing.

fr: Elle est revenue les mains vides.
en: She came back empty-handed.
note: Revenir uses être → past participle agrees with feminine subject: revenue.

fr: J'ai failli lui dire la vérité.
en: I almost told him the truth.
note: Faillir + infinitif in passé composé. Lui dire = to tell him/her (indirect object).

fr: Quand j'étais enfant, on n'avait pas tout ça.
en: When I was a child, we didn't have all of this.
note: Imparfait for habitual past states. On n'avait pas = we didn't have. Notice tout ça = all of this/that.

fr: Il parlait sans s'arrêter.
en: He was talking non-stop.
note: Imparfait for ongoing past action. Sans s'arrêter = without stopping. Literally 'without stopping himself'.

fr: On aurait dit qu'elle n'avait rien à faire.
en: You'd have thought she had nothing to do.
note: On aurait dit que = it seemed like / you'd have thought. N'avait rien à faire = had nothing to do.

fr: Elle savait très bien ce qu'elle faisait.
en: She knew very well what she was doing.
note: Savoir is usually in imparfait (state of knowing), not passé composé, unless there's a sudden realization.

fr: Ils n'arrêtaient pas de se contredire.
en: They kept contradicting each other.
note: Ne pas arrêter de + infinitif = to keep doing (repeated/annoying action). Se contredire = to contradict each other.

fr: La situation devenait de plus en plus compliquée.
en: The situation was becoming more and more complicated.
note: De plus en plus + adjectif = more and more. Imparfait shows gradual change.

fr: Je ne m'y attendais vraiment pas.
en: I really wasn't expecting that.
note: S'attendre à = to expect. Y replaces à + thing. Ne … vraiment pas = really not.

fr: On vivait comme si tout allait bien.
en: We were living as if everything was fine.
note: Comme si + imparfait = as if (contrary to reality). Both verbs in imparfait.

fr: On verra bien ce que ça donnera.
en: We'll see how it turns out.
note: On verra is extremely common — dismissive or expectant. Ce que ça donnera = what it'll come to.

fr: Il finira par comprendre, tôt ou tard.
en: He'll eventually understand, sooner or later.
note: Finir par + infinitif in the future = will eventually. Tôt ou tard = sooner or later.

fr: Ça ne changera rien à la situation.
en: It won't change anything about the situation.
note: Ne … rien with future: the negation wraps the conjugated verb. À la situation = regarding the situation.

fr: Vous devrez justifier votre décision.
en: You will have to justify your decision.
note: Devoir in future = will have to / must. Used to express obligation in the future.

fr: Elle saura s'en sortir, j'en suis sûr.
en: She'll manage, I'm sure of it.
note: Savoir + infinitif = to know how to / to be able to. S'en sortir = to manage / get out of a situation.

fr: Les prix vont continuer à augmenter.
en: Prices are going to keep rising.
note: Futur proche: aller + infinitif. Used for near future or certain outcomes. Common in speech over futur simple.

fr: Ça ne se passera pas comme ça.
en: It won't go down like that.
note: Se passer = to happen/go. Comme ça = like that. Strong expression of refusal or prediction.

fr: À ta place, j'aurais réagi différemment.
en: If I were you, I would have reacted differently.
note: À ta place = if I were you. Aurais réagi = conditionnel passé (would have + past participle).

fr: Ce serait bien de s'expliquer calmement.
en: It would be good to talk things through calmly.
note: Ce serait bien de + infinitif = it would be good to. Polite suggestion using conditionnel.

fr: Il faudrait qu'on revoit nos priorités.
en: We would need to reconsider our priorities.
note: Il faudrait que + subjonctif. Softer than il faut que — suggests rather than demands.

fr: J'aurais préféré qu'elle m'en parle avant.
en: I would have preferred she told me beforehand.
note: Conditionnel passé expresses regret. Qu'elle m'en parle = subjonctif in the subordinate clause.

fr: Ça pourrait être bien pire, tu sais.
en: It could be a lot worse, you know.
note: Pourrait = conditionnel of pouvoir. Bien pire = much worse. Tu sais softens the remark.

fr: On devrait en discuter avec toute l'équipe.
en: We should discuss it with the whole team.
note: Devrait = conditionnel of devoir = should. More polite/advisive than the indicative doit.

fr: Sans ton aide, je n'y serais jamais arrivé.
en: Without your help, I never would have managed.
note: Sans + nom replaces a si-clause. Y arriver = to manage/succeed at it.

fr: Je n'aurais jamais pensé que c'était si loin.
en: I would never have thought it was so far.
note: Conditionnel passé expresses surprise or counterfactual. Si loin = so far.

fr: Je doute qu'il soit vraiment sincère.
en: I doubt he's genuinely sincere.
note: Douter que triggers subjonctif. Soit = subjonctif of être. Express doubt → subjonctif.

fr: Il est important qu'on se comprenne.
en: It's important that we understand each other.
note: Il est important que + subjonctif. Se comprendre = to understand each other. Comprenne = subjonctif of comprendre.

fr: Je veux qu'il sache ce que j'en pense.
en: I want him to know what I think about it.
note: Vouloir que + subjonctif. Sache = subjonctif of savoir. Ce que j'en pense = what I think of it.

fr: Bien qu'elle soit fatiguée, elle continue.
en: Even though she's tired, she keeps going.
note: Bien que always triggers subjonctif — a concession. Soit = subjonctif of être.

fr: C'est la meilleure décision qu'on ait prise.
en: It's the best decision we've made.
note: After a superlative, subjonctif is expected. Ait prise = passé du subjonctif of prendre.

fr: Il faut que tu le fasses maintenant.
en: You have to do it now.
note: Il faut que + subjonctif. Fasses = subjonctif of faire. Very common obligation pattern.

fr: Je ne crois pas qu'il ait compris.
en: I don't think he understood.
note: Negative croire que triggers subjonctif. Ait compris = passé du subjonctif — past event viewed with doubt.

fr: Elle avait déjà pris sa décision avant qu'on parle.
en: She had already made up her mind before we spoke.
note: Plus-que-parfait = had + past participle. Used for an action completed before another past action.

fr: Il n'avait jamais vu ça de sa vie.
en: He had never seen anything like it in his life.
note: Ne … jamais with plus-que-parfait: wraps the auxiliary. De sa vie = in his life (emphatic).

fr: On s'était mis d'accord, pourtant.
en: We had agreed, though.
note: Se mettre d'accord = to agree/reach agreement. Reflexive → être as auxiliary. Pourtant = yet/though.

fr: Si j'avais su, j'aurais agi autrement.
en: If I had known, I would have acted differently.
note: Classic si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé structure. Expresses regret about the past.

fr: Ils n'avaient pas prévu que ça tournerait si mal.
en: They hadn't anticipated it would turn out so badly.
note: Prévoir → past participle prévu. Que + conditionnel in the subordinate clause for future-in-the-past.

fr: Ce n'est pas aussi simple que tu le crois.
en: It's not as simple as you think.
note: Aussi … que = as … as. Que tu le crois = than you think (le is a neuter pronoun referring to the idea).

fr: La plupart des gens ne s'en rendent pas compte.
en: Most people don't realise it.
note: La plupart de takes a plural verb. Ne s'en rendent pas compte — en replaces de ça.

fr: Ça ne me regarde pas, mais quand même…
en: It's none of my business, but even so…
note: Ça ne me regarde pas = it's not my concern. Quand même = even so / still (very versatile word).

fr: On n'a pas le droit de se taire.
en: We have no right to stay silent.
note: Avoir le droit de = to have the right to. Se taire = to be quiet/silent (reflexive, irregular).

fr: Je suis tout à fait d'accord avec vous.
en: I completely agree with you.
note: Être d'accord avec = to agree with. Tout à fait = completely, absolutely. Formal register.

fr: Il ne s'agit pas de ça du tout.
en: That's not what this is about at all.
note: Il s'agit de = it's about / it concerns. Always impersonal (always il). Du tout = at all.

fr: On a tendance à oublier les détails importants.
en: We tend to forget the important details.
note: Avoir tendance à + infinitif = to tend to. Very common in discussion and argumentation.

fr: Ça vaut la peine d'essayer, non ?
en: It's worth trying, isn't it?
note: Valoir la peine de + infinitif = to be worth doing. Non ? as a tag question, common in speech.

fr: On a mis trois heures pour arriver à destination.
en: It took us three hours to reach our destination.
note: Mettre + time + pour + infinitif = to take (time) to do. À destination = to the destination.

fr: Il faut réserver à l'avance si on veut un bon tarif.
en: You need to book in advance if you want a good rate.
note: Il faut + infinitif = one must / you need to. À l'avance = in advance. Tarif = fare/rate.

fr: Franchement, je ne vois pas où tu veux en venir.
en: Honestly, I don't see what you're getting at.
note: Vouloir en venir à = to be getting at / leading to. Franchement opens bluntly — signals you're about to push back. Essential oral argument phrase.

fr: Attends, laisse-moi finir.
en: Wait, let me finish.
note: Laisse-moi + infinitif = let me do. Imperative of laisser. Short, assertive — used to reclaim your turn in a conversation or debate.

fr: C'est pas faux, mais c'est pas toute la vérité non plus.
en: That's not wrong, but it's not the whole truth either.
note: C'est pas = spoken contraction of ce n'est pas. Non plus = either (in negatives). Classic partial concession — agree a bit, then push back.

fr: Dans un sens, t'as raison. Dans un autre, pas vraiment.
en: In a way, you're right. In another way, not really.
note: Dans un sens = in a way. T'as = spoken form of tu as. Pas vraiment = not really. This sentence teaches the oral concession-then-retract move.

fr: Honnêtement, je sais pas trop quoi penser.
en: Honestly, I'm not really sure what to think.
note: Je sais pas = spoken je ne sais pas — dropped ne is normal in casual speech. Quoi penser = what to think. Use this when you need time to form an opinion.

fr: Ça dépend vraiment de comment on voit les choses.
en: It really depends on how you look at things.
note: Ça dépend de comment = it depends on how. Spoken structure — written French would say de la façon dont. Useful for nuancing a position without committing.

fr: Tu vois ce que je veux dire ?
en: You know what I mean?
note: Classic comprehension check in spoken French. Tu vois alone = you see? / you get it? Ce que je veux dire = what I mean. Use it after explaining something complex.

fr: C'est là où ça devient intéressant.
en: That's where it gets interesting.
note: C'est là où = that's where. Used to flag that you're about to make your key point. Signals to the examiner that the substance is coming.

fr: Je vais être honnête, ça m'agace profondément.
en: I'm going to be honest, it really irritates me.
note: Je vais être honnête = I'm going to be honest — spoken opener that signals strong feeling. Agacer = to irritate/annoy. Profondément adds intensity.

fr: Pour être franc, je m'attendais à mieux.
en: To be frank, I expected better.
note: Pour être franc = to be frank. A softened way to criticise. Je m'attendais à mieux = I expected better — polite but pointed.

fr: En gros, ce qu'il dit c'est que personne n'est responsable.
en: Basically, what he's saying is that nobody is responsible.
note: En gros = basically / roughly speaking. Very common oral discourse marker. Ce qu'il dit c'est que = what he's saying is that — summarising someone else's position.

fr: Voilà exactement le problème.
en: That's exactly the problem.
note: Voilà used as a pointer/emphasis — 'there it is / that's it'. Exactement = exactly. Short, punchy. Use it when the other person has just proved your point for you.

fr: Oui, mais encore faut-il que ce soit applicable.
en: Yes, but it still has to be applicable.
note: Encore faut-il que + subjonctif = but one still needs to / the thing is it still has to be. Elegant way to accept a point while introducing a practical objection.

fr: Je comprends ce que tu dis, mais faut pas exagérer non plus.
en: I get what you're saying, but let's not go overboard either.
note: Faut pas = spoken il ne faut pas. Exagérer = to exaggerate / go overboard. Non plus = either. Classic moderating move in oral debate.

fr: Là tu me poses une colle.
en: Now you've got me stumped.
note: Poser une colle à quelqu'un = to stump someone / ask a trick question. Useful when you don't know the answer — buys time and sounds natural rather than lost.

fr: C'est une bonne question, j'y avais pas vraiment pensé.
en: That's a good question, I hadn't really thought about it.
note: Classic time-buyer in oral exams. J'y avais pas pensé = I hadn't thought about it (y replaces à ça). Dropped ne is natural in speech.

fr: Comment dire… c'est compliqué à expliquer.
en: How can I put it… it's complicated to explain.
note: Comment dire = how can I put it / how shall I say. A filler that signals you're searching for the right words — completely normal in oral French, not a sign of weakness.

fr: Disons que c'est pas exactement ce à quoi je m'attendais.
en: Let's just say it's not exactly what I was expecting.
note: Disons que = let's say / let's just say. Softens a complaint or criticism. Frequently used when you want to express disappointment without being too direct.

fr: Dans un premier temps, je pensais que c'était une bonne idée.
en: At first, I thought it was a good idea.
note: Dans un premier temps = at first / initially. Oral discourse marker signalling you're about to describe an evolving opinion — implies a 'but then' is coming.

fr: Sur le moment, j'ai rien dit, mais ça m'a choqué.
en: At the time I said nothing, but it shocked me.
note: Sur le moment = at the time / in the moment. Rien dit — dropped ne in speech. Describes a delayed emotional reaction — very natural storytelling structure.

fr: T'aurais pas pu le dire avant ?
en: Couldn't you have said that earlier?
note: T'aurais = tu aurais — spoken contraction. Conditionnel passé used as reproach. Rising intonation makes it a question. Very natural expression of frustration.

fr: Ça m'aurait évité bien des soucis.
en: It would have saved me a lot of trouble.
note: Éviter à quelqu'un + nom = to spare someone something. Bien des = a good deal of / many. Conditionnel passé for regret about an outcome.

fr: Je veux bien admettre que j'avais tort.
en: I'm willing to admit I was wrong.
note: Vouloir bien + infinitif = to be willing to / to be happy to. More gracious than je dois admettre. Shows good faith in an argument — earns points in oral exams.

fr: On va pas se mentir, c'est loin d'être idéal.
en: Let's not kid ourselves, it's far from ideal.
note: On va pas se mentir = let's not fool ourselves / let's be real. Informal but effective opener. Loin d'être = far from being — useful intensifier.

fr: Au final, c'est toujours les mêmes qui paient.
en: In the end, it's always the same people who pay the price.
note: Au final = in the end / ultimately — spoken alternative to finalement. Les mêmes = the same ones. Strong opinioned closer — good for wrapping up a point.

fr: C'est bien beau de le dire, encore faut-il le faire.
en: It's all very well to say it, but you still have to do it.
note: C'est bien beau de + infinitif = it's all very well to. Encore faut-il = but one still has to. Classic structure for challenging empty promises or abstract plans.

fr: J'ai du mal à croire qu'on en est encore là.
en: I find it hard to believe we're still at this point.
note: Avoir du mal à + infinitif = to find it hard to. En être là = to be at this point / stage. Expresses exasperation about a situation that hasn't progressed.

fr: Ça fait réfléchir, quand même.
en: It does make you think, though.
note: Faire réfléchir = to make you think. Quand même as a closer = still / nevertheless. Short sentence used to acknowledge weight of an argument you're partially conceding.

fr: Je vais pas prétendre que c'est simple.
en: I'm not going to pretend it's simple.
note: Je vais pas = spoken je ne vais pas. Prétendre que = to claim/pretend that. Used to signal honesty before making a concession or difficult point.

fr: On peut pas tout réduire à ça.
en: You can't reduce everything to that.
note: On peut pas = spoken on ne peut pas. Réduire à = to reduce to. Used when someone oversimplifies — pushes back against reductive arguments.

fr: C'est justement ce point-là qui me pose problème.
en: That's exactly the point that's a problem for me.
note: Justement = precisely / exactly — used to pinpoint something. Ce point-là = that specific point (-là is a demonstrative reinforcer). Poser problème = to be a problem.

fr: Je rejoins ton point de vue sur ce sujet.
en: I agree with your point of view on that.
note: Rejoindre le point de vue de quelqu'un = to agree with someone's view. More nuanced than être d'accord — implies you've considered it and arrived at the same position.

fr: À mon avis, on fait fausse route.
en: In my opinion, we're on the wrong track.
note: À mon avis = in my opinion — most natural oral marker for opinion. Faire fausse route = to be on the wrong track / barking up the wrong tree. Idiomatic.

fr: J'aurais tendance à dire que non.
en: I'd be inclined to say no.
note: Avoir tendance à + infinitif in conditionnel = I'd be inclined to. Softer and more nuanced than a flat non — shows measured thinking rather than reflex reaction.

fr: C'est pas tant le résultat qui m'inquiète, c'est le processus.
en: It's not so much the result that worries me, it's the process.
note: C'est pas tant X que Y = it's not so much X as Y. Redirects emphasis from one element to another. Very effective in oral argumentation — shows precision of thought.

fr: Ça reste à prouver.
en: That remains to be proven.
note: Rester à + infinitif = remains to be done. Short and sceptical — challenges an unproven claim without being aggressive. Good for maintaining your position under pressure.

fr: Je vois pas en quoi ça change quoi que ce soit.
en: I don't see how that changes anything at all.
note: En quoi = in what way / how (formal embedded question). Quoi que ce soit = anything at all. Strong dismissal structure — challenges the relevance of a point.

fr: Mettons les choses au clair.
en: Let's get things straight.
note: Mettre les choses au clair = to set the record straight. First person plural imperative (mettons) = let's. Used to reset a conversation that has gone off track.

fr: Je tiens quand même à souligner un point important.
en: I do want to highlight one important point.
note: Tenir à + infinitif = to insist on / really want to. Quand même here = still / nonetheless. Souligner = to highlight/underline. Signals your key argument is coming.

fr: Ça me semble un peu exagéré, je dois dire.
en: That seems a bit exaggerated to me, I have to say.
note: Sembler + adjectif = to seem. Je dois dire as a tag = I have to say — softens the criticism. Un peu = a bit — makes the pushback less confrontational.

fr: On s'éloigne du sujet là.
en: We're going off topic here.
note: S'éloigner du sujet = to go off topic / stray from the subject. Là as a discourse marker signals immediacy. Use it to steer a conversation back on track.

fr: C'est exactement ça le nœud du problème.
en: That's exactly the crux of the problem.
note: Le nœud du problème = the crux / heart of the problem. Lit. 'the knot of the problem'. Signals you're naming the core issue — strong move in structured oral argument.

fr: Je veux pas dire ça méchamment, hein.
en: I don't mean that nastily, you know.
note: Hein = right? / you know? — a tag seeking validation. Méchamment = nastily / meanly. Used to soften a blunt criticism — shows social awareness in oral interaction.

fr: Ça n'a rien à voir avec ce dont on parlait.
en: That has nothing to do with what we were talking about.
note: N'avoir rien à voir avec = to have nothing to do with. Ce dont on parlait = what we were talking about (dont = of which, required after parler de).

fr: Au bout du compte, on revient toujours au même problème.
en: When all is said and done, we always come back to the same problem.
note: Au bout du compte = when all is said and done / ultimately. Synonymous with en fin de compte. Good oral closer — wraps up a circular discussion.

fr: Je maintiens ce que j'ai dit.
en: I stand by what I said.
note: Maintenir ce que = to stand by / maintain what. Je maintiens = I maintain/stand by. Short and firm — use it when pressured to back down from a position.

fr: T'as qu'à essayer toi-même si tu penses que c'est si simple.
en: You just try it yourself if you think it's that simple.
note: T'as qu'à / tu n'as qu'à + infinitif = you just have to / all you need to do is. Here it's used ironically as a challenge. Very colloquial — shows you understand spoken register.

fr: Je vais pas entrer dans les détails maintenant.
en: I'm not going to go into the details right now.
note: Entrer dans les détails = to go into detail. Used to stay at a high level deliberately — signals awareness of time and relevance in oral production.

fr: C'est une chose de le savoir, c'en est une autre de l'appliquer.
en: Knowing it is one thing, applying it is another.
note: C'est une chose de… c'en est une autre de… = it's one thing to… it's another to…. C'en = ce en — contracted. Elegant contrast structure for oral argumentation.

fr: Tout ça pour dire que la situation est loin d'être résolue.
en: All that to say the situation is far from resolved.
note: Tout ça pour dire que = all that to say / the bottom line is. Used after a long explanation to deliver the conclusion. Very natural spoken summarising phrase.

fr: Je vais reformuler parce que j'ai l'impression qu'on se comprend pas.
en: I'll rephrase because I get the feeling we're not understanding each other.
note: Reformuler = to rephrase. J'ai l'impression que = I get the feeling that / I have the impression that. Key oral repair strategy — shows communicative awareness.

fr: Faut pas oublier non plus que ça a un coût.
en: We also shouldn't forget that it has a cost.
note: Faut pas = spoken il ne faut pas. Non plus = either / also (in context of adding a point). Introduces a practical counterargument — cost, consequence, or trade-off.

fr: C'est un sujet qui me tient à cœur.
en: It's a subject close to my heart.
note: Tenir à cœur = to be close to one's heart / to care about deeply. Use this in the oral when asked about a topic you want to engage with — signals genuine investment.

fr: J'aurais beaucoup de choses à dire là-dessus.
en: I could say a lot about that.
note: Avoir beaucoup à dire sur = to have a lot to say about. Conditionnel signals it's a considered choice to limit yourself. Good opener that establishes you have depth on a topic.

fr: Prenons un exemple concret pour illustrer ça.
en: Let's take a concrete example to illustrate that.
note: Prenons un exemple = let's take an example. First person plural imperative. Concret = concrete / specific. Signals you're about to back up an abstract point — examiners love this.

fr: Sans vouloir te contredire, je pense que tu fais une erreur.
en: Without wanting to contradict you, I think you're making a mistake.
note: Sans vouloir + infinitif = without wanting to. Diplomatic opener before disagreement. Faire une erreur = to make a mistake. Polite but direct — perfect oral register.

fr: Je peux pas m'empêcher de penser que quelque chose cloche.
en: I can't help thinking something's off.
note: Ne pas pouvoir s'empêcher de = can't help doing. Quelque chose cloche = something's off/wrong (lit. 'something rings wrong'). Signals intuitive scepticism.

fr: Soit dit en passant, c'est pas la première fois.
en: By the way, it's not the first time.
note: Soit dit en passant = by the way / let it be said in passing. Fixed expression using subjonctif of être. Used to add a pointed aside — often implies a pattern of behaviour.

fr: C'est bien ça le paradoxe.
en: That's exactly the paradox.
note: C'est bien ça = that's exactly it / that's precisely it. Bien here is an intensifier, not 'well'. Short and definitive — used to name the contradiction at the heart of an argument.

fr: On peut pas nier que la tendance va dans ce sens.
en: You can't deny that the trend is going in that direction.
note: On peut pas nier que + indicatif = you can't deny that. Indicatif follows because the speaker accepts the fact. Used to concede a point strategically before pivoting.

fr: Elle ne fait que se plaindre depuis ce matin.
en: She's done nothing but complain since this morning.
note: Ne faire que + infinitif = to do nothing but / to only do. Very common in spoken French to express frustration.

fr: Plus on attend, moins on a de chances.
en: The longer we wait, the fewer chances we have.
note: Plus… moins… = the more… the less…. A parallel comparative structure — both clauses in the same tense.

fr: Je n'en reviens pas qu'il ait dit ça.
en: I can't believe he said that.
note: Ne pas en revenir = to not be able to believe something. Triggers subjonctif in the subordinate clause.

fr: Il a beau essayer, rien ne change.
en: No matter how hard he tries, nothing changes.
note: Avoir beau + infinitif = no matter how much one does something, it doesn't help. Unique structure with no direct English equivalent.

fr: Ça fait trois ans qu'elle travaille ici.
en: She's been working here for three years.
note: Ça fait + durée + que + présent = has been doing for X time. French uses present tense where English uses present perfect continuous.

fr: Je suis en train de relire mes notes.
en: I'm in the middle of reviewing my notes.
note: Être en train de + infinitif = to be in the middle of doing. The true French present continuous — emphasises the action is happening right now.

fr: Tant qu'on ne sait pas la vérité, on ne peut rien faire.
en: As long as we don't know the truth, we can't do anything.
note: Tant que + présent = as long as. The condition and result are both in present tense.

fr: Il ne s'en sort pas sans aide.
en: He can't manage without help.
note: S'en sortir = to manage / cope. En refers to the situation. Sans aide = without help.

fr: On a du mal à se comprendre en ce moment.
en: We're having trouble understanding each other right now.
note: Avoir du mal à + infinitif = to have trouble doing. Se comprendre = to understand each other (reciprocal reflexive).

fr: Elle tient bon malgré tout.
en: She's holding firm despite everything.
note: Tenir bon = to hold firm / stand one's ground. Malgré tout = despite everything — very common filler in argument and opinion.

fr: Ça ne sert à rien de s'énerver.
en: There's no point getting worked up.
note: Ne servir à rien de + infinitif = there's no point doing. S'énerver = to get annoyed/worked up (reflexive).

fr: Je tiens à préciser que ce n'était pas mon idée.
en: I want to make it clear that it wasn't my idea.
note: Tenir à + infinitif = to insist on / be keen to do. More emphatic than vouloir — signals the speaker feels strongly.

fr: On ne peut pas se permettre de rater cette occasion.
en: We can't afford to miss this opportunity.
note: Se permettre de + infinitif = to afford to / allow oneself to. Works for money or figuratively for situations.

fr: Il est hors de question qu'on abandonne maintenant.
en: There's no question of us giving up now.
note: Il est hors de question que + subjonctif = it's out of the question that. Strong refusal structure — the subjonctif follows automatically.

fr: Autant le dire clairement : ça ne marchera pas.
en: We might as well say it plainly: it won't work.
note: Autant + infinitif = we might as well / one may as well. Used to introduce a blunt or obvious statement.

fr: Il avait à peine fermé les yeux qu'il s'endormit.
en: He had barely closed his eyes when he fell asleep.
note: À peine + plus-que-parfait… que + passé simple = barely… when. The plus-que-parfait shows the first completed action.

fr: On n'avait jamais vu une telle confusion.
en: We had never seen such confusion.
note: Tel/telle = such a. Agreement with the noun: telle confusion (feminine). Plus-que-parfait with jamais expresses a first-time event.

fr: Elle avait tout prévu sauf ça.
en: She had planned for everything except that.
note: Prévoir → irregular past participle prévu. Sauf = except. Simple but very natural structure.

fr: Ils s'étaient réconciliés avant la fin de la semaine.
en: They had made up before the end of the week.
note: Reflexive + être auxiliary. Réconciliés agrees with the plural subject. Avant la fin de = before the end of.

fr: Je n'avais pas réalisé à quel point c'était difficile.
en: I hadn't realised how difficult it was.
note: À quel point = how much / to what extent. Used in indirect questions and exclamations. Very TCF-relevant structure.

fr: Le projet avait échoué faute de financement.
en: The project had failed for lack of funding.
note: Faute de + nom = for lack of / due to the absence of. No article after faute de. Formal, used in written and spoken B2+.

fr: Elle avait beau insister, personne ne l'écoutait.
en: No matter how much she insisted, nobody listened to her.
note: Avoir beau + infinitif in the past = no matter how much she tried. The futility is set in the past context here.

fr: Nous avions supposé qu'il serait là.
en: We had assumed he would be there.
note: Plus-que-parfait + que + conditionnel in subordinate clause = future-in-the-past. Very common in narrative.

fr: Tout s'était passé exactement comme prévu.
en: Everything had gone exactly as planned.
note: Comme prévu = as planned / as expected. Se passer uses être. Exactement adds precision.

fr: Il n'avait pas eu le temps de s'expliquer.
en: He hadn't had time to explain himself.
note: Avoir le temps de + infinitif = to have time to. S'expliquer = to explain oneself / have it out.

fr: Si tu travaillais un peu plus, tu réussirais.
en: If you worked a bit harder, you would succeed.
note: Classic si + imparfait → conditionnel présent. Hypothetical present/future — the situation could still change.

fr: Si on avait su, on aurait agi autrement.
en: If we had known, we would have acted differently.
note: Si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé. Expresses regret — the window has closed, nothing can be changed.

fr: Même si j'avais voulu, je n'aurais pas pu.
en: Even if I had wanted to, I wouldn't have been able to.
note: Même si + plus-que-parfait → conditionnel passé. Même si = even if — stronger concession than si alone.

fr: À moins que tu ne changes d'avis, c'est fini.
en: Unless you change your mind, it's over.
note: À moins que + subjonctif = unless. Note the optional expletive ne after à moins que — not a negation, just formal usage.

fr: Quand bien même tu t'excuserais, ce ne serait pas suffisant.
en: Even if you were to apologise, it wouldn't be enough.
note: Quand bien même + conditionnel = even if. More emphatic than même si. Formal register, common in argumentation.

fr: Il aurait mieux valu ne rien dire.
en: It would have been better to say nothing.
note: Il aurait mieux valu + infinitif = it would have been better to. Expresses hindsight regret. Valoir is impersonal here.

fr: On aurait pu éviter tout ça.
en: We could have avoided all of this.
note: Conditionnel passé of pouvoir = could have. Implies criticism or regret. Tout ça = all of this.

fr: Je n'aurais pas dû lui faire confiance.
en: I shouldn't have trusted him.
note: Conditionnel passé of devoir = should have (regret). Faire confiance à = to trust. Very common expression.

fr: Si j'étais à sa place, je démissionnerais.
en: If I were in his position, I would resign.
note: Si + imparfait → conditionnel. Être à la place de = to be in someone's position. Démissionner = to resign.

fr: Pourvu qu'elle arrive à temps.
en: Let's hope she arrives on time.
note: Pourvu que + subjonctif = let's hope that / provided that. Expresses a strong wish with underlying anxiety.

fr: Avant qu'il parte, dis-lui la vérité.
en: Before he leaves, tell him the truth.
note: Avant que + subjonctif = before. Always triggers subjonctif when subjects differ. Parte = subjonctif of partir.

fr: Quoiqu'il dise, je ne le crois plus.
en: Whatever he says, I don't believe him anymore.
note: Quoique + subjonctif = although / whatever. Dise = subjonctif of dire. Ne… plus = no longer / not anymore.

fr: Je ne pense pas qu'elle ait eu tort.
en: I don't think she was wrong.
note: Negative penser que + subjonctif. Ait eu = passé du subjonctif of avoir. Avoir tort = to be wrong.

fr: Il faut trouver une solution avant que la situation empire.
en: We need to find a solution before the situation gets worse.
note: Avant que + subjonctif. Empirer = to worsen. Faut + infinitif here (no que) = impersonal obligation without specifying who.

fr: Bien que ce soit difficile, ça vaut la peine.
en: Even though it's difficult, it's worth it.
note: Bien que + subjonctif = even though. Valoir la peine = to be worth it (no de when used alone).

fr: Je cherche quelqu'un qui sache cuisiner.
en: I'm looking for someone who can cook.
note: Subjonctif after chercher quelqu'un qui — the person is hypothetical, not identified. If they existed, it would be indicative.

fr: C'est dommage qu'on ne se soit pas vus plus tôt.
en: It's a shame we didn't see each other sooner.
note: C'est dommage que + subjonctif. Se soit vus = passé du subjonctif of se voir (reflexive, agrees with subject).

fr: Pour que ça marche, il faut que tout le monde s'implique.
en: For it to work, everyone needs to get involved.
note: Pour que + subjonctif = so that / in order that. Two subjonctifs in one sentence — marche and s'implique.

fr: Je ne crois pas qu'on puisse faire mieux.
en: I don't think we can do better.
note: Puisse = subjonctif of pouvoir. Negative croire que triggers subjonctif. Common in debate and evaluation.

fr: Elle travaille ici depuis qu'elle a fini ses études.
en: She's been working here since she finished her studies.
note: Présent + depuis que + passé composé. The main clause is present (ongoing), the depuis que clause marks when it started.

fr: Au fur et à mesure qu'il parlait, je comprenais mieux.
en: The more he talked, the better I understood.
note: Au fur et à mesure que = as / gradually as. Both verbs in imparfait — simultaneous ongoing actions in the past.

fr: Dès qu'il aura signé, on pourra commencer.
en: As soon as he signs, we'll be able to start.
note: Dès que + futur antérieur → futur simple. French requires futur antérieur after time conjunctions — English uses simple present.

fr: Quand tu auras fini, dis-le-moi.
en: When you're done, let me know.
note: Quand + futur antérieur — same rule as dès que. The completed future action precedes the request. Often tested in TCF.

fr: Il ne répondra que lorsqu'il sera prêt.
en: He'll only answer when he's ready.
note: Lorsque + futur — another time conjunction requiring future tense. Ne… que = only. Not a true negation — restricts rather than denies.

fr: Une fois qu'on aura réglé ça, on passera à autre chose.
en: Once we've sorted this out, we'll move on.
note: Une fois que + futur antérieur. Passer à autre chose = to move on — essential phrase for discussions and arguments.

fr: Ça fera deux heures qu'on attend quand il arrivera.
en: We'll have been waiting two hours by the time he arrives.
note: Ça fera + durée + que + futur = will have been doing for X time by then. The French way of expressing duration in future context.

fr: Il vaut mieux prévenir que guérir.
en: Prevention is better than cure.
note: Il vaut mieux + infinitif = it is better to. Prévenir = to prevent. A set expression worth knowing for TCF opinion tasks.

fr: On a eu beau chercher, on n'a rien trouvé.
en: No matter how hard we looked, we found nothing.
note: Avoir beau + infinitif in passé composé. Ne… rien trouvé — rien between auxiliary and past participle.

fr: Elle a fini par accepter, à contrecœur.
en: She ended up accepting, reluctantly.
note: Finir par + infinitif in passé composé = ended up doing. À contrecœur = reluctantly / against one's will.

fr: Je m'en suis sorti grâce à ton aide.
en: I got through it thanks to your help.
note: S'en sortir = to get through / manage. Reflexive → être auxiliary. Grâce à = thanks to (positive cause, unlike à cause de).

fr: On s'est rendu compte trop tard qu'on avait tort.
en: We realised too late that we were wrong.
note: Mixed tenses: passé composé + imparfait in subordinate clause. Avoir tort = to be wrong. Trop tard = too late.

fr: Il a prétendu ne pas être au courant.
en: He claimed not to know about it.
note: Prétendre + infinitif = to claim to do. Negation before infinitif: ne pas être. Être au courant = to be aware / in the know.

fr: La réunion a duré bien plus longtemps que prévu.
en: The meeting lasted much longer than planned.
note: Bien plus… que prévu = much more than planned. Bien intensifies comparisons. Que prévu = than expected/planned.

fr: On s'est mis d'accord sur l'essentiel.
en: We agreed on the essentials.
note: Se mettre d'accord sur = to agree on. L'essentiel = the essential part / what matters most. Common in professional contexts.

fr: Les gens avaient l'habitude de se parler dans la rue.
en: People used to talk to each other in the street.
note: Avoir l'habitude de + infinitif = to be used to doing / to usually do. Imparfait here emphasises the repeated past habit.

fr: Chaque fois qu'il essayait, quelque chose l'en empêchait.
en: Every time he tried, something prevented him.
note: Chaque fois que + imparfait = every time (repeated past). Empêcher quelqu'un de = to prevent someone from doing.

fr: On se demandait ce qu'on allait bien pouvoir faire.
en: We were wondering what on earth we were going to do.
note: Se demander ce que = to wonder what. Bien pouvoir adds exasperation — 'what on earth'. Indirect question in imparfait.

fr: Plus elle expliquait, moins je comprenais.
en: The more she explained, the less I understood.
note: Plus… moins… with imparfait = ongoing parallel contrast in the past. Both clauses stay in imparfait.

fr: Il prétendait tout savoir, mais c'était faux.
en: He claimed to know everything, but it was false.
note: Prétendre in imparfait = habitual claiming. Tout savoir = to know everything. C'était faux = it was untrue/false.

fr: À l'époque, personne ne s'en préoccupait.
en: Back then, nobody was concerned about it.
note: À l'époque = back then / at the time. Se préoccuper de = to be concerned about. En replaces de ça.

fr: On n'avait pas les mots pour le décrire.
en: We didn't have the words to describe it.
note: Avoir les mots pour = to have the words to. Simple but expressive. Le décrire — le is a neuter pronoun referring to the whole situation.

fr: Elle agissait comme si elle ne savait rien.
en: She was acting as if she knew nothing.
note: Agir comme si + imparfait = to act as if. The imparfait after comme si signals a hypothesis contrary to reality.

fr: D'ici là, il faudra trouver une autre solution.
en: By then, we'll need to find another solution.
note: D'ici là = by then / between now and then. Il faudra = futur of falloir. Expresses future necessity.

fr: Ça finira par s'arranger, j'en suis convaincu.
en: It'll work out in the end, I'm convinced of it.
note: Finir par + infinitif in futur. S'arranger = to sort itself out. J'en suis convaincu = I'm convinced of it.

fr: Il ne le dira jamais, tu le sais bien.
en: He'll never say it, you know that perfectly well.
note: Ne… jamais with futur: wraps the conjugated verb. Tu le sais bien = you know very well — used to challenge the listener.

fr: Quoi qu'il arrive, on fera face ensemble.
en: Whatever happens, we'll face it together.
note: Quoi qu'il arrive = whatever happens. Arrive is subjonctif. The main clause is futur. Faire face = to face / cope.

fr: Tôt ou tard, la vérité éclatera.
en: Sooner or later, the truth will come out.
note: Tôt ou tard = sooner or later. Éclater = to burst out / explode — used figuratively for scandals and truths coming to light.

fr: Ça ne durera pas, crois-moi.
en: It won't last, trust me.
note: Durer = to last. Crois-moi = trust me / believe me — imperative used as a parenthetical. Common in spoken argument.

fr: Elle aura bientôt oublié tout ça.
en: She'll have forgotten all about it soon.
note: Futur antérieur = will have done. Used here not after a conjunction but to express a future completed state — often with bientôt or dans peu de temps.

fr: On verra bien qui avait raison.
en: We'll see who was right.
note: On verra = we'll see — dismissive or confident depending on tone. Qui avait raison = who was right (imparfait in embedded clause).

fr: Il convient de rappeler que les résultats sont mitigés.
en: It is worth pointing out that the results are mixed.
note: Il convient de + infinitif = it is fitting to / worth doing. Formal register, frequent in written French and TCF production tasks.

fr: Force est de constater que rien n'a changé.
en: One has to admit that nothing has changed.
note: Force est de constater que = one must admit / it must be acknowledged that. Fixed formal expression — very useful in essays and TCF.

fr: Quand on y pense, c'est assez inquiétant.
en: When you think about it, it's quite worrying.
note: Quand on y pense = when you think about it. Y replaces à ça. Conversational opener that signals reflection.

fr: La question qui se pose est de savoir comment y remédier.
en: The question that arises is how to remedy it.
note: La question qui se pose = the question that arises. De savoir comment = of knowing how. Formal essay structure.

fr: Il ne faut pas confondre vitesse et précipitation.
en: One must not confuse speed with haste.
note: A French proverb-like expression. Confondre A et B = to confuse A with B. Précipitation = haste/rashness. High register.

fr: Ça soulève une question importante.
en: That raises an important question.
note: Soulever une question = to raise a question. More precise than poser une question — implies the issue is complex or controversial.

fr: On ne peut pas nier que la situation s'est améliorée.
en: One cannot deny that the situation has improved.
note: Ne pas nier que + indicatif — when the speaker accepts the fact, indicatif follows. If they doubted it, subjonctif would follow.

fr: Ce n'est pas en restant silencieux qu'on résout les problèmes.
en: It's not by staying silent that problems get solved.
note: Ce n'est pas en + gérondif que = it's not by doing X that Y happens. Emphatic structure stressing the wrong method.

fr: Il est grand temps qu'on prenne ça au sérieux.
en: It's high time we took this seriously.
note: Il est grand temps que + subjonctif = it's high time that. Expresses urgency and mild reproach. Prendre au sérieux = to take seriously.

fr: Mieux vaut tard que jamais, comme on dit.
en: Better late than never, as they say.
note: Mieux vaut + nom/infinitif = it's better to. A set expression. Comme on dit = as they say — used to quote common wisdom without fully endorsing it.

fr: En admettant que ce soit vrai, qu'est-ce qu'on fait ?
en: Assuming that's true, what do we do?
note: En admettant que + subjonctif = assuming that / even granting that. Used to concede a point hypothetically without accepting it fully.

fr: Sans qu'il s'en aperçoive, tout avait changé.
en: Without him realising it, everything had changed.
note: Sans que + subjonctif = without (someone) doing. S'apercevoir de = to notice / become aware of. Different subject from main clause.

fr: Aussi surprenant que cela puisse paraître, c'est vrai.
en: As surprising as it may seem, it's true.
note: Aussi + adjectif + que + subjonctif = as X as it may seem. Formal concession structure. Puisse = subjonctif of pouvoir.

fr: À supposer qu'on accepte, que se passera-t-il ensuite ?
en: Supposing we agree, what happens next?
note: À supposer que + subjonctif = supposing that. Hypothetical concession, similar to en admettant que. Useful in negotiation contexts.

fr: Quel que soit le résultat, on continuera.
en: Whatever the result, we'll carry on.
note: Quel que soit + nom = whatever X is. Quel agrees with the noun: quel (masc.) or quelle (fem.). Very common in formal speech.

fr: Où qu'il aille, il emporte ses problèmes avec lui.
en: Wherever he goes, he takes his problems with him.
note: Où que + subjonctif = wherever. Aille = subjonctif of aller. Emporter = to take/carry with one.

fr: À force de répéter la même chose, on finit par se faire entendre.
en: By repeating the same thing over and over, you eventually get heard.
note: À force de + infinitif = by dint of / through repeated doing. Se faire entendre = to be heard / make oneself heard (causative reflexive).

fr: C'est à force d'essayer qu'on progresse.
en: It's through repeated trying that you improve.
note: C'est… que cleft structure for emphasis. À force de + infinitif again — drilling this structure in a different context.

fr: Il a suffi d'un mot pour tout gâcher.
en: It only took one word to ruin everything.
note: Il a suffi de + nom + pour + infinitif = it only took X to do Y. Suffire = to be enough. Gâcher = to ruin/spoil.

fr: Il suffit de peu pour que tout bascule.
en: It takes very little for everything to tip over.
note: Il suffit de + peu/infinitif + pour que + subjonctif. Basculer = to tip / fall / shift suddenly. Figurative use, common in political commentary.

fr: C'est en forgeant qu'on devient forgeron.
en: Practice makes perfect.
note: C'est en + gérondif + que = it's by doing X that Y happens. A French proverb. Lit. 'It's by forging that one becomes a blacksmith.' TCF loves testing this structure.

fr: Faute de mieux, on acceptera ce compromis.
en: For lack of anything better, we'll accept this compromise.
note: Faute de mieux = for lack of anything better. Fixed expression. Compromis = compromise — essential vocabulary for argument topics.

fr: À en juger par les résultats, on a réussi.
en: Judging by the results, we succeeded.
note: À en juger par = judging by. Fixed formal expression. En refers to what follows. Very common in written French and TCF.

fr: Quoi qu'on en dise, ça reste une belle réussite.
en: Whatever people say, it remains a great achievement.
note: Quoi qu'on en dise = whatever people say. Dise = subjonctif of dire. Réussite = achievement/success.

fr: Il ne saurait être question d'ignorer ce problème.
en: There can be no question of ignoring this problem.
note: Ne saurait + infinitif = cannot possibly / there is no way. Conditionnel of savoir used as a strong negation — very formal register.

fr: Tant s'en faut qu'on ait trouvé la solution idéale.
en: We are far from having found the ideal solution.
note: Tant s'en faut que + subjonctif = far from / it is far from the case that. Formal expression of understatement. High-register TCF vocabulary.

fr: Je n'ai pas pu m'empêcher de rire.
en: I couldn't help laughing.
note: Ne pas pouvoir s'empêcher de + infinitif = can't help doing. Double negation: ne… pas + s'empêcher. Very natural spoken expression.

fr: Elle s'est laissé convaincre, finalement.
en: She let herself be convinced in the end.
note: Se laisser + infinitif = to let oneself be done to. Note: laissé does NOT agree when followed by an infinitive — invariable rule.

fr: On a du mal à croire qu'il s'agit du même pays.
en: It's hard to believe this is the same country.
note: Avoir du mal à croire que = to find it hard to believe. Il s'agit de = it is about / this is. Indicatif follows because there's no doubt expressed.

fr: Il ne manquait plus que ça.
en: That's all we needed. / That's the last thing we needed.
note: Il ne manquait plus que ça = that's all we needed (ironic). Fixed expression of exasperation. Imparfait here is the standard form regardless of current tense.

fr: Je ne suis pas sans savoir que ça pose des problèmes.
en: I'm not unaware that it causes problems.
note: Ne pas être sans savoir que = to not be unaware / to well know. Double negative for understatement — acknowledges the problem while softening the admission.

fr: On est loin du compte.
en: We're nowhere near / far off the mark.
note: Être loin du compte = to be far off / nowhere near enough. Idiomatic. Often used when numbers, progress, or expectations don't match reality.

fr: Ça tombe bien, j'allais justement t'appeler.
en: Good timing, I was just about to call you.
note: Ça tombe bien = good timing / that works out well. Aller + infinitif in imparfait = was about to (imminent past action that may or may not have happened).

fr: Il n'y a pas de quoi en faire un drame.
en: There's no need to make a big deal of it.
note: Il n'y a pas de quoi + infinitif = there's no reason to. En faire un drame = to make a drama of it. Common when downplaying a situation.

fr: C'est ce qui nous différencie des autres.
en: That's what sets us apart from the others.
note: Ce qui + verbe = what (subject relative pronoun). Différencier de = to differentiate / set apart from. Useful for comparison and opinion tasks.

fr: Il n'est pas dit qu'on réussira du premier coup.
en: It's not certain we'll succeed on the first try.
note: Il n'est pas dit que + futur/subjonctif = it's not certain that. Expresses uncertainty about an outcome. Du premier coup = on the first try.
